LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT OPM PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT SOLICITED HER BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA AND ENTERED INTO A RELATIONSHIP REQUIRING CONTINUING CONTACTS WITH HER IN CALIFORNIA AND THAT THE ALLEGED MISCONDUCT IS SUFFICIENTLY RELATED TO THOSE CONTACTS SUCH THAT REQUIRING DEFENDANT TO DEFEND ITSELF IS NOT UNFAIR. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Default Prove-Up Hearing As To Defendant David O'Brien, Only.
Off Calendar May-06-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Daniel Berko, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Judy Macks and Judy Macks as Trustee of the Judy Macks 2004 Revocable Trust.
No appearance by defendant David O'Brien.
Default was entered against defendant David O'Brien on April 15, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on May 5, 2010.
Dismissals without prejudice as to defendants Gary Glascoe and OPM Property Management, Inc. on the Second Amended Complaint were filed on May 5, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Judy Macks and Judy Macks as Trustee of the Judy Macks 2004 Revocable Trust, and against the Defaulted Defendant David O'Brien as follows:

Damages/Principal - $300,000.00.
Costs - $545.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $300,545.00.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT O P M PROPERTY MGMT, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT SOLICITED HER BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA AND ENTERED INTO A RELATIONSHIP REQUIRING CONTINUING CONTACTS WITH HER IN CALIFORNIA AND THAT THE ALLEGED MISCONDUCT IS SUFFICIENTLY RELATED TO THOSE CONTACTS SUCH THAT REQUIRING DEFENDANT TO DEFEND ITSELF IS NOT UNFAIR. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/X-KS
